title: transport: connect() reader task silently eats post-close frames
description: In core/src/transport.rs connect() reader task, when the relay closes the connection, in_tx is dropped and RelayConn.rx.recv() returns None. This is correct Tokio behavior. However, there is no ClientEvent::Disconnected variant and no documentation that None from rx means disconnect. RelayConnRetry callers receive ClientEvent::Reconnecting explicitly; RelayConn callers only get a silent None. Callers that loop on Some(_) without handling None will silently stop processing events. This is a documentation/API clarity risk for new callers of RelayConn.
